title: Arrays, Dynamic Sequences, and Locality · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Design an event buffer with predictable indexing, insertion, and memory behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an array-backed sequence with operation traces. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain indexing and cache-local traversal from contiguous logical storage.
Trace capacity growth and amortized append behavior through resize events.
Compare indexed access, search, middle insertion, deletion, slicing, and copying costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
